content:
MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: Normal volatility (Vol: 27.1%) with broad participation (Breadth 7/8 sectors positive). Market tone favors cyclicals and high-beta names after a week of broad gains.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Holding & Investment and Energy & Chemicals are top performers: SAHOL (1W: +5.44% | 1M: -13.73%) RECOVERY, DOHOL (1W: +6.94% | 1M: -3.63%) RECOVERY, PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND, SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY. Construction & REIT is also active with TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY and ENKAI (1W: +4.52% | 1M: -8.46%) RECOVERY.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Banking & Finance is the clear laggard: AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND and HALKB (1W: -4.33% | 1M: -13.50%) BELOW TREND remain under pressure despite the risk-on backdrop.

STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND, SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY, PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +7.07% | 1M: -6.05%) RECOVERY.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND, AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, OYAKC (1W: -2.04% | 1M: -15.43%) BELOW TREND, ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION, ALARK (1W: -3.49% | 1M: -21.23%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
High 1W momentum concentrated in TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND, SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY, PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND and TOASO (1W: +7.07% | 1M: -6.05%) RECOVERY.

SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking remains out of favor and acting as a drag on the market; key names AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND and ISCTR (1W: -1.05% | 1M: -22.48%) BELOW TREND show sustained weakness. Visibility on margins and asset quality is limited, keep banks underweight until technical repair.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry and autos show a selective recovery: TOASO (1W: +7.07% | 1M: -6.05%) RECOVERY, EREGL (1W: +0.71% | 1M: -5.02%) RECOVERY and FROTO (1W: +1.11% | 1M: -13.76%) RECOVERY are catching flows while ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION is digesting gains. Positioning should favor names with clear order/earnings visibility.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy is constructive with TUPRS (1W: +2.13% | 1M: +18.00%) ABOVE TREND and PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND leading; SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY shows volatile rebound. OYAKC (1W: -2.04% | 1M: -15.43%) BELOW TREND is a reminder of idiosyncratic weakness; favor cash-flow resilient names.

PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Risk-on stance: overweight Holding & Investment, Energy & Chemicals and select cyclicals (autos, construction) that are leading the rebound; trim banks and weak consumer staples. Keep size disciplined and use earnings/catalyst windows to add rather than chase momentum.

NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +2.13% | 1M: +18.00%) ABOVE TREND, TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION and GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND for sector flow and earnings/volume signals.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Holding & Investment, Energy & Chemicals and Construction & REIT—these sectors are driving breadth and will determine whether the risk-on move broadens or fades. Monitor rotation between cyclicals and defensives.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 reaction to near-term resistance at last week’s highs and support at the 20-day moving average; a sustained break above the highs would confirm continuation while a drop below the 20-day MA signals consolidation. Key catalysts: domestic macro prints and policy commentary, FX moves and global risk sentiment/commodity prices.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Risk-on bias with normal volatility and 7/8 sectors positive; rotation favors holdings, energy and selected cyclicals while banks remain the primary weak spot. Trade selectively into strength around catalysts and avoid size in structurally weak banking and consumer names until technical repair.
